content.full_text:
July 9, 1979

Mrs. Hedy Govenar
Zanelli Associates
6464 Sunset Boulevard, Suite 550
Los Angeles, CA 90028

Dear Mrs. Govenar:

Thank you for your letter dated July 2, 1979 in which you asked
two questions with respect to the activities of Zanelli and
Associates under the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform
Act. Your questions were:

1. Exactly what are the prohibitions which affect
Zanelli Associates as the lobbyist employer of
Hedy Govenar, lobbyist?

2. If a separate set of books and a separate bank
account is established under the name of Zanelli
Associates Lobbying Account, then is this the only
account which may be audited by the FPPC?
Currently, there are Orange County and Los Angeles
accounts.

With respect to your first question, if a lobbyist is a partner
(other than a limited partner) in a business entity, a share-
holder in a professional corporation, holds 10% or more of the
common stock of a corporation, or is a member, partner, employee
or agent of a retained entity which is an employer of a lobbyist
the prohibitions set forth in Government Code Sections 86202,
86203 and 86205 are applicable with respect to the assets of the
business entity, corporation or retained entity. (2 Cal Admin.
Code Section 18619(h) )

Government Code Section 86202 specifies that it shall be unlawful
to make a contribution, or to act as an agent or intermediary
in the making of any contribution or to arrange for the making of
any contribution to a state candidate, an elected state officer,
a legislative official or an agency official.

Government Code Section 86203 specifies that it shall be unlawful
to make gifts directly or indirectly to a state candidate, an
elected state officer, a legislative official or an agency official
aggregating more than $10 in a calendar month, or to act as an
agent or intermediary in the making of any gift, or to arrange for

Mrs. Hedy Govenar                    -2-                    July 9, 1979

the making of any gift by any other person.

Government Code Section 82205 provides that no lobbyist shall:
    (a) Do anything with the purpose of placing any elected state
officer, legislative official, agency official, or state candidate
under personal obligation to him or to his employer;
    (b) Deceive or attempt to deceive any elected state officer,
legislative official, agency official, or state candidate with
regard to any material fact pertinent to any pending or proposed
legislative or administrative action;
    (c) Cause or influences the introduction of any bill or amend-
ment thereto for the purpose of thereafter being employed to
secure its passage or defeat;
    (d) Attempt to create a fictitious appearance of public favor
or disfavor of any proposed legislative or administrative action
or to cause any communication to be sent to any elected state
officer, legislative official, agency official, or state candidate
in the name of any fictitious person or in the name of any real
person, except with the consent of such real person;
    (e) Represent falsely either directly or indirectly, that he
can control the official action of any elected state officer,
legislative official, or agency official;
    (f) Accept or agree to accept any payment in any way contingent
upon the defeat, enactment or outcome of any proposed legislative
or administrative action.

Accordingly, Zannelli Associates is prohibited from making contributions and gifts of more than $10 and the activities described above
so long as the firm is an employer of a lobbyist.

With respect to your second question, if the firm establishes a
separate set of books and a separate checking account for lobbying
activities, an audit of the firm lobbying activities would be
limited to those books and checking account. In addition, establishment of separate lobbying accounts would facilitate any
future audits. However, if the audit disclosed that lobbying activities had been commingled in other accounts an auditor would
have authority to audit those additional accounts.

If I can be of further assistance, please contact me.

                                                    Sincerely,


                                                    J. Richard Eichman
                                                    Accounting Specialist
JRE: ms

Z
ZANELLI ASSOCIATES
Jerry Zanelli, Hedy Govenar, Dan Wooldridge

July 2, 1979

Mr. Richard Blohman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Rich:

This is a followup to our May 9 meeting at which we discussed
FPPC regulations regarding a member of a campaign management firm
becoming a lobbyist, and specifically how our firm should proceed
with plans to offer lobbying as part of our services.

We are now in the processing of negotiating with several
prospective clients and would like some written clarification
from you on two points.

1. Exactly what are the prohibitions which affect
   Zanelli Associates as the lobbyist employer of
   Hedy Govenar, lobbyist?

2. If a separate set of books and a separate bank
   account is established under the name of Zanelli
   Associates Lobbying Account, then is this the only
   account which may be audited by the FPPC? Currently,
   there are Orange County and Los Angeles accounts.

Thank you for your help. I will be bringing appropriate
forms for your review to Sacramento so that we make sure we are
proceding correctly within the guidelines of the Lobbying Dis-
closure Provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Sincerely,

Hedy Govenar

CAMPAIGN MANAGEMENT AND GOVERNMENTAL AFFAIRS
6464 Sunset Boulevard, Suite 550, Los Angeles, California 90028, 213-462-6555

sections.question:
1. Exactly what are the prohibitions which affect Zanelli Associates as the lobbyist employer of Hedy Govenar, lobbyist?

2. If a separate set of books and a separate bank account is established under the name of Zannelli Associates Lobbying Account, then is this the only account which may be audited by the FPPC? Currently, there are Orange County and Los Angeles accounts.
sections.conclusion: Zannelli Associates is prohibited from making contributions and gifts of more than $10 and the activities described above so long as the firm is an employer of a lobbyist. If the firm establishes a separate set of books and a separate checking account for lobbying activities, an audit of the firm lobbying activities would be limited to those books and checking account. However, if the audit disclosed that lobbying activities had been commingled in other accounts an auditor would have authority to audit those additional accounts.
sections.question_synthetic: The questioner asks what prohibitions apply to Zanelli Associates as an employer of a lobbyist, and whether establishing separate lobbying books and bank accounts would limit FPPC audits to only those accounts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: As a lobbyist employer, Zanelli Associates is subject to contribution and gift prohibitions and other restrictions under Government Code Sections 86202, 86203, and 86205. Separate lobbying books and accounts would limit initial audits to those accounts, but auditors retain authority to examine other accounts if lobbying activities are commingled.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated July 2, 1979) and the FPPC response letter (dated July 9, 1979). Minor spelling variation in company name between request and response (Zannelli vs Zanelli). All key sections clearly identified and extracted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Exactly what are the prohibitions which affect Zanelli Associates as the lobbyist employer of Hedy Govenar, lobbyist?

2. If a separate set of books and a separate bank account is established under the name of Zannelli Associates Lobbying Account, then is this the only account which may be audited by the FPPC? Currently, there are Orange County and Los Angeles accounts.

CONCLUSION: Zannelli Associates is prohibited from making contributions and gifts of more than $10 and the activities described above so long as the firm is an employer of a lobbyist. If the firm establishes a separate set of books and a separate checking account for lobbying activities, an audit of the firm lobbying activities would be limited to those books and checking account. However, if the audit disclosed that lobbying activities had been commingled in other accounts an auditor would have authority to audit those additional accounts.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Zanelli Associates regarding prohibitions applicable to the firm as an employer of lobbyist Hedy Govenar, and advises that separate lobbying accounts would facilitate audits while noting auditors can examine other accounts if activities are commingled.
